title: What Is Financial Literacy?
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: In which areas of your life do you most need financial literacy today—earning, spending, borrowing, or protecting yourself?
What is one small financial decision you will handle more carefully after this game?

stages.prompt: Which statement best describes financial literacy?

options.label: A skill only rich people need
options.reflection: Financial literacy is important for everyone, especially when money is limited. It helps you stretch and protect what you have.

options.label: The ability to make smart money decisions
options.reflection: Correct. Financial literacy means understanding money well enough to make better choices when earning, saving, spending, and borrowing.

options.label: Finding shortcuts to make quick profit
options.reflection: Quick profits are often risky. Financial literacy is about long‑term stability, not get‑rich‑quick tricks.

options.label: Only about investing in the stock market
options.reflection: Investing is just one part. Financial literacy also covers budgeting, debt, emergencies, and protecting yourself from fraud.

stages.prompt: Which of these is a sign of good financial literacy?

options.label: Spending freely without tracking anything
options.reflection: Not tracking at all makes it hard to know where your money is going or how to improve your situation.

options.label: Balancing today’s needs with future goals
options.reflection: Yes. Good financial literacy helps you enjoy today while still planning for emergencies and long‑term goals.

options.label: Copying whatever friends do with money
options.reflection: Everyone’s situation is different. Blindly copying others can lead you into decisions that don’t suit your income or responsibilities.

options.label: Avoiding all formal finance and only using cash
options.reflection: Formal finance (banks, regulated lenders, insurance) gives you protections and opportunities that cash alone cannot.
